summary: Split the former sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3B34 range into hsd_3B34 and new hsd_3B5C translation units, added nonmatching JPEG encode/decode C and associated static table data, updated splits/configure wiring, and preserved hsd_803B5C2C as a claimed 100% match while improving multiple fuzzy matches.

key_files.role: Adjusted extab, extabindex, text, data, and sdata2 ranges for hsd_3B34 and introduced hsd_3B5C split ranges.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt hsd_3B34 end moved to text 0x803B5C4C and hsd_3B5C added from text 0x803B5C4C to 0x803B7230

key_files.role: Registered sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c as a NonMatching object in the build configuration.
Expanded from stub-like content into nonmatching JPEG encode-side C, static JPEG table data, externs, helper routines, and retained hsd_803B5C2C in the first TU.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added lbl_803B9670, lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, hsd_803B3408, fn_803B376C and related JPEG code

key_files.role: Header surface changed for the split/JPEG routines.
New nonmatching TU for the later JPEG/decode-side helper cluster beginning at hsd_803B5C4C.
evidence_refs: pr_body: hsd_3B5C begins at hsd_803B5C4C with decode-side helper cluster

what_changed: The former hsd_3B34 text range ending at 0x803B7230 was split at 0x803B5C4C, leaving hsd_3B34 as 0x803B3408-0x803B5C4C and adding hsd_3B5C as 0x803B5C4C-0x803B7230.
The split also divided extab at 0x80005690, extabindex at 0x800058E8, data at 0x80431090, and sdata2 at 0x804DEB90 according to the diff.

Added nonmatching C for JPEG encode/decode routines in both TUs, including generated-looking temporaries, address-style function names, M2C_FIELD accesses, extern data references, and JPEG table arrays.
Added hsd_3B5C.c to configure.py as a NonMatching object.
PR body claims hsd_803B5C2C stayed 100% matching and the two new TUs had fuzzy scores of 84.855255% for hsd_3B34 and 84.95146% for hsd_3B5C.
Bot report recorded 3 new data/rodata matches and 15 unmatched-item improvements, including function fuzzy improvements in hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C.

smart_moves.text: Used multiple boundary signals for the TU split rather than only a function boundary: adjacent extab/extabindex cut points, clean data-section separation, sdata2 separation, and hsd_803B5C2C sitting immediately before hsd_803B5C4C.
Kept the new hsd_3B5C object explicitly NonMatching in configure.py, matching the PR's stated status instead of implying full code match.
Documented verification commands in the PR body, including targeted pre-commit over touched source/header files and a configure plus ninja build.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A plausible TU split can be supported by a bundle of section-boundary evidence: function boundary, extab/extabindex cuts, data cluster boundary, and sdata2 boundary all lining up.
evidence_refs: pr_body: split at hsd_803B5C4C described as natural TU boundary
raw_diff: hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C split ranges in splits.txt

decomp_lessons.lesson: When introducing nonmatching decomp for a large generated-looking cluster, address-style names and temp_rXX/var_rXX locals may be acceptable intake state if the PR is explicitly scoped as fuzzy/nonmatching progress, but they should not be treated as final naming style.
evidence_refs: pr_body: add nonmatching C and fuzzy match percentages

decomp_lessons.lesson: For split work, keeping build configuration and splits metadata in the same PR as the new TU lets report tooling attribute data and fuzzy improvements to the correct unit.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C items

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for uncertain functions and data symbols, including hsd_803B3408, fn_803B376C, lbl_803B9670, lbl_80430C40, and lbl_80431090 references.
evidence_refs: pr_body: split/data rationale names lbl_80430C40 and lbl_80431090
naming_conventions.notes: This aligns with conservative naming for uncertain semantics, but the PR does not establish a new naming standard.

naming_conventions.convention: Generated/decompiler local names such as temp_r22, var_r10, var_ctr, and unused_arg4 remain in the nonmatching C.
naming_conventions.notes: Evidence supports only that these appeared in this nonmatching PR, not that they are preferred final names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: TU split boundary was chosen using section metadata: extab/extabindex cut points, text boundary at 0x803B5C4C, data split at 0x80431090, and sdata2 split at 0x804DEB90.
Large static JPEG table clusters were materialized in C as byte/word arrays tied to split data ranges, producing bot-reported .data and .rodata matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static const u8 lbl_803B9670, static u8 lbl_80430C40, static u8 lbl_80430C80
text_corpus: bot report 3 new matches for hsd_3B5C .data, hsd_3B34 .data, hsd_3B34 .rodata

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used M2C_FIELD and raw buffer-style access through HSD_804D2648_BUF in nonmatching JPEG routines as bridge code for fuzzy progress.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards treat M2C_FIELD/raw pointer math as bridge code; this PR evidence does not prove recovered types for those accesses.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PR reported targeted formatting/lint and full build verification: pre-commit on touched baselib source/header files and python configure.py --wrapper build/tools/wibo && ninja.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Report for GALE01 showed matched data increased to 39.28% (+0.26%, +3126 bytes), with 3 new data/rodata matches and 15 improvements in unmatched items across hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: hsd_803B5C4C TU boundary extab extabindex data sdata2
sysdolphin baselib JPEG hsd_3B34 hsd_3B5C M2C_FIELD

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata and changed-file counts
human_pr_text.md: full PR body with rationale, match status, and verification
notes: PR body provides unusually explicit TU split rationale and verification commands.
Diff evidence is truncated, so full contents of hsd_3B34.c, hsd_3B5C.c, and hsd_3B34.h were not inspected from the loaded slice.
No human review comments are available; reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
The PR claims hsd_803B5C2C is 100% matching and reports fuzzy scores; loaded evidence does not include independent objdiff output for that specific claim.
Bot report supports data/rodata matches and fuzzy improvements but is not a substitute for source-level acceptance of bridge tactics such as M2C_FIELD.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use aligned section cut points to justify TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2488 supports a TU split at hsd_803B5C4C by combining function-boundary evidence with adjacent extab/extabindex cut points and clean data/sdata2 cluster separation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep new split objects explicitly NonMatching when fuzzy C is introduced
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2488 added hsd_3B5C.c to configure.py as Object(NonMatching, ...) while the PR described the new JPEG C as nonmatching and reported fuzzy percentages.

source_update_candidates.title: hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C split ranges after PR #2488
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2488 split sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34.c at hsd_803B5C4C and introduced sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c with text 0x803B5C4C-0x803B7230, data 0x80431090-0x804316C0, and sdata2 0x804DEB90-0x804DEC00.
source_update_candidates.reason: The diff directly changes split ownership metadata, but curator should verify against current source before adding or updating path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: JPEG table cluster ownership around lbl_80430C40 and lbl_80431090
source_update_candidates.text: PR body states hsd_3B34 owns the first static JPEG table cluster through lbl_80430C40 / .sdata2 804DEB90, while hsd_3B5C begins with a separate large table at lbl_80431090 and its own .sdata2 constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt data and sdata2 boundaries
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data ownership note; should be checked against symbols/current splits before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: TU split rationale should cite multiple boundary types when available
source_update_candidates.text: A reviewable TU split rationale can cite aligned text, extab/extabindex, data, and sdata2 boundaries plus local function-cluster behavior, as PR #2488 did for hsd_803B5C4C.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with the loaded data-sections-and-tu-splits standard, but should be curated as a possible clarification rather than automatically promoted.

rejection_notes: Do not promote M2C_FIELD or HSD_804D2648_BUF raw buffer access as accepted final style; PR evidence only supports their use in nonmatching bridge code.
Do not infer semantic names for JPEG routines or tables beyond address-style labels present in the PR.
Do not treat the claimed fuzzy percentages or hsd_803B5C2C 100% match as independently verified beyond the PR body and bot report in the loaded slice.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
